hypothesis: Defensive rate/dollar ETFs already present in the fixed OHLCV snapshots (IEF, TLT, UUP) might improve the candidate pool without adding noisy single-stock exposure.
why_not_llm_soft_ranking: LLM soft-ranking remains production-sample limited, so this tested a deterministic production/backtest universe expansion instead.

market_regime_summary.mid_weak: rotation-heavy bull after accepted allocation stack
market_regime_summary.old_thin: mixed-to-weak older tape with weakest win rate

delta_metrics.notable_trade_change: mid_weak added/replaced one TLT trend target but displaced better existing opportunity; total PnL fell.

gate4_basis: Rejected and rolled back. The only EV improvement was +0.0005 in mid_weak, aggregate PnL fell by $440.21, and no Gate 4 threshold was met.
mechanism_insight: Adding defensive ETFs to the tradeable universe creates extra candidates but does not release material alpha under the current slot, heat, and ranking stack. A low-volatility defensive target can still be opportunity-cost negative.

llm_metrics.blocker_relation: LLM soft-ranking data was insufficient, so this deterministic universe alpha was tested instead.

rejection_reason: Failed Gate 4: aggregate EV was effectively unchanged and aggregate PnL regressed by $440.21.
do_not_repeat_without_new_evidence: Adding IEF/TLT/UUP as a simple defensive ETF universe expansion
Treating defensive ETF targets as alpha without measuring opportunity cost
Broad ETF universe expansion without a state/event discriminator and production watchlist parity
next_retry_requires: A state discriminator showing when defensive ETF continuation should compete for scarce slots
Forward evidence that defensive ETF candidates improve opportunity cost
A ranking rule that prevents low-volatility ETFs from displacing higher-EV A/B candidates
